grid_logic.label: Grid Logic Puzzle
grid_logic.promptTemplate:
Design a Grid Logic puzzle game.

Mechanics:
- Grid size: 4x4 or 8x8 squares, tiles 48x48px.
- Move speed: 0.15s per tile transition.
- Matching requirement: 3 connected items or numeric overlap.
- Score multiplier: starts at 1.0x, adds +0.5x for cascades within 0.8s.

Mandatory Juice:
- Burst effect: 12-15 sparks on match, expanding to 60px over 0.3s (cubic-bezier easing).
- Screen shake: 2px magnitude for 0.1s on combo > 3.
- SFX: Web Audio sine waves at 440Hz for select, quick glissando 600-800Hz (0.1s ADSR) for match.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No complex menus before gameplay.
- No punishing timers in the first 5 levels.
- Avoid visually noisy backgrounds distracting from the grid.
- Do not use physics engines for grid snapping.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Threes meets Tetris, highly tactile, clicky, and immediately responsive.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Clean grid layout
- [ ] Clear selected state (border/glow)
- [ ] Distinct match animation
- [ ] Score display with floating numbers
- [ ] Restart button
- [ ] LocalStorage save state
- [ ] Progression curve scaling over time

Controls:
Mobile: swipe to shift tiles (min distance 30px). Desktop: WASD/Arrows.

Concrete details below:

hyper_casual.label: Hyper Casual Reflex
hyper_casual.promptTemplate:
Design a Hyper Casual reflex game.

Mechanics:
- Core loop: 1-tap interaction.
- Obstacle speed: 120px/s moving left, scales by +10px/s per point.
- Player gravity: 980px/s^2, jump impulse 350px/s.
- Hitbox: tightly bound to player sprite (radius 16px).

Mandatory Juice:
- Pop effect: 8 white rings expanding and fading over 0.2s on score increase.
- Screen shake: 4px magnitude for 0.15s on collision.
- SFX: Web Audio square wave 220Hz jumping to 440Hz for tap, sawtooth 100Hz crunch for failure.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No multi-button controls.
- No lore or story text.
- Avoid slow start speeds (get into action in 1 second).
- Do not use complex textures; stick to solid flat colors or gradients.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Flappy Bird, frustratingly fair, punchy, and instantly restartable.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Single tap input listener
- [ ] Object pooling for obstacles
- [ ] Collision detection routine
- [ ] High score LocalStorage tracking
- [ ] Flash effect on failure
- [ ] Large centralized UI score counter

Controls:
Mobile: Tap anywhere on screen. Desktop: Spacebar or Mouse Click.

Concrete details below:

idle_clicker.label: Idle Clicker
idle_clicker.promptTemplate:
Design an Idle Clicker incremental game.

Mechanics:
- Base click value: 1 point, upgrades add +1 or multipliers (1.5x).
- Auto-generators: tick every 1.0s, starting at 0.5 points/tick.
- Upgrade cost scaling: Cost = Base * (1.15 ^ quantity owned).
- Offline progress simulation capability.

Mandatory Juice:
- Floating text: '+1' rising 30px upwards, fading over 0.8s with ease-out.
- Pop effect: Button scales up 1.05x on pointer down, springs back on release.
- SFX: Web Audio triangle wave 800Hz gentle ping on manual tap, 600Hz soft tick for auto-generation.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No punishment for going idle.
- Avoid requiring precise timing.
- No linear cost scaling (must be exponential).
- Do not clutter the screen with unpurchasable future items early on.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Cookie Clicker, satisfyingly numerical with cascading visual feedback.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Giant central click target
- [ ] Upgrades panel (scrollable)
- [ ] Number formatting (1K, 1M, 1B)
- [ ] Tick-based resource loop
- [ ] Visual indicator of current points per second
- [ ] Deep LocalStorage auto-save every 5s

Controls:
Mobile: Rapid tapping on large element. Desktop: Rapid mouse clicking.

Concrete details below:

card_battle.label: Card Battle Strategy
card_battle.promptTemplate:
Design a Card Battle strategy game.

Mechanics:
- Energy/Mana: Starts at 3 per turn, resets each round.
- Deck: Draw 5 cards per turn. Max hand size 10.
- Damage/Block: Exact numerical subtraction. Armor mitigates damage 1:1.
- Card costs: Range from 0 to 3 energy.

Mandatory Juice:
- Hover state: Card scales 1.1x and translates Y -10px in 0.1s.
- Burst effect: 20 spark elements flying toward target upon card release.
- Screen shake: 3px for 0.2s on high damage impact (>10).
- SFX: Web Audio filtered noise (whoosh) for card draw, 200Hz punch for impact.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No real-time action mechanics.
- Avoid hidden math; show exact damage projections.
- No overly small card text (ensure readability on mobile).
- Avoid complex multi-targeting in v1.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Slay the Spire, methodical, clearly readable, with satisfying kinetic card weight.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Draggable cards with snapback
- [ ] Enemy intent indicators
- [ ] Health and Armor bars
- [ ] Energy counter UI
- [ ] Discard and Draw pile counters
- [ ] Turn progression state machine

Controls:
Mobile: Drag card to target/play area. Desktop: Click and drag.

Concrete details below:

tower_defense.label: Tower Defense
tower_defense.promptTemplate:
Design a Tower Defense game.

Mechanics:
- Grid or Waypoint path: Enemies move at 50-100px/s.
- Tower Range: 80-150px radius.
- Fire Rate: 0.5s to 2.0s between shots.
- Projectile Speed: 300px/s with simple predictive aiming or tracking.

Mandatory Juice:
- Impact spark: Small 10px circular expanding flash on projectile hit.
- Tower recoil: Turret sprite shifts back 2px and springs forward on firing.
- SFX: Web Audio short burst noise (150ms) for firing, 300Hz low pop for enemy defeat.
- Enemy squeeze: Enemies squash 0.8x on Y axis when taking damage.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- Avoid maze-building in v1 (use fixed paths instead).
- No overly long waves without economy drops.
- Do not make tower placement confusing (snap to grid or fixed nodes).
- Avoid unreadable overlapping enemy clusters.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Bloons TD, visually busy but readable, with clear upgrade paths and satisfying impacts.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Waypoint pathing system
- [ ] Radius indicator on hover
- [ ] Economy/Money system
- [ ] Multiple tower types (e.g., fast, splash, slow)
- [ ] Wave spawning manager
- [ ] Health tracking per enemy

Controls:
Mobile: Tap node, tap tower to build. Desktop: Click node, click tower.

Concrete details below:

quiz.label: Quiz / Word
quiz.promptTemplate:
Design a Quiz or Word game.

Mechanics:
- Question/Word bank: 30+ items minimum, embedded in code.
- Time per question: Optional 10-20s soft timer (no hard fail in v1).
- Feedback states: right / wrong / close (yellow/orange near-miss).
- Streak counter: bonus +1 per correct, resets on wrong.

Mandatory Juice:
- Letter pop: Each tile flips with rotateY 0->180deg over 0.4s on reveal.
- Burst effect: 8 confetti specks on correct, falling 80px over 0.6s.
- Screen shake: 3px for 0.1s on wrong.
- SFX: Web Audio sine 660Hz for correct, 220Hz square for wrong, 440Hz blip for typing.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No tiny touch targets (each key min 44x44px).
- Avoid unfair pixel-hunt or trick wording.
- Do not punish at start (warm-up easy questions).
- Avoid hard timer that interrupts reading.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Wordle, calm, satisfying, sharable result strings.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] On-screen keyboard with key states (correct/present/absent)
- [ ] Tile flip animation on submit
- [ ] Streak counter UI
- [ ] Restart button
- [ ] LocalStorage best streak
- [ ] Result share string (emoji squares)

Controls:
Mobile: Tap on-screen keyboard. Desktop: Physical keyboard + on-screen mirror.

Concrete details below:

top_down.label: Top-Down Action / Roguelite
top_down.promptTemplate:
Design a Top-Down 2D action game.

Mechanics:
- Player speed: 180px/s, no acceleration ramp (instant input).
- Enemy spawn rate: starts at 0.8/s, scales +0.1/s every 10s.
- Auto-attack interval: 0.6s, range 120px, damage 5.
- HP: player 20, basic enemy 3, scaling with wave index.

Mandatory Juice:
- Hit spark: 6 radial dashes 12px outward over 0.15s on enemy damage.
- Screen shake: 2px on player hit, 5px on player defeat.
- Knockback: enemies pushed 20px on hit over 0.1s.
- SFX: Web Audio square 880Hz blip for shot, sawtooth 110Hz pop for enemy down, low 60Hz thud for player damage.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No complex weapon menus mid-run.
- Avoid bullet-sponge enemies in early waves.
- Do not block input during animations.
- Avoid invisible projectiles (always have a clear sprite/trail).

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Vampire Survivors, escalating chaos, quick mastery loop, satisfying numeric pop-ups.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] WASD/joystick input layer
- [ ] Collision (circle vs circle)
- [ ] Wave/spawn manager
- [ ] HP bar (top of canvas)
- [ ] Damage number floats on enemy hit
- [ ] Game over + restart screen

Controls:
Mobile: Virtual joystick bottom-left, auto-fire. Desktop: WASD or arrows, auto-fire.

Concrete details below:

platformer.label: 2D Platformer
platformer.promptTemplate:
Design a 2D Platformer.

Mechanics:
- Player: 32x48px sprite/box, run speed 220px/s, jump impulse 520px/s, gravity 1600px/s^2.
- Coyote time: 100ms after walking off ledge.
- Jump buffer: 80ms input grace before landing.
- Variable jump: short tap 0.4 of full impulse, hold for full.
- Collision: AABB, ground probe 2px below feet.
- Level: 1-3 hand-built screens with platforms + a goal flag.

Mandatory Juice:
- Land puff: 6 dust specks at feet, drifting 16px over 0.25s.
- Screen shake: 3px for 0.1s on landing from > 200px fall.
- SFX: Web Audio square 660Hz for jump, sine 120Hz thud for land, triangle 880Hz for pickup, sawtooth 80Hz crunch for death.
- Death effect: player breaks into 8 small squares spinning outward over 0.4s.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No instant-kill spikes without windup.
- Avoid floaty jumps (gravity must feel weighty).
- Do not punish input mid-air with no recovery.
- Avoid invisible collision walls.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Celeste, tight inputs, forgiving timing windows, micro-deaths-then-restart loop.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] AABB collision pass
- [ ] Coyote + jump-buffer timers
- [ ] Level data structure (array or JSON)
- [ ] Camera follow (lerp 0.1)
- [ ] Death + respawn flow
- [ ] Win flag + level transition

Controls:
Mobile: 3-button overlay (left, right, jump). Desktop: Arrow keys + Space/Z for jump.

Concrete details below:

visual_novel.label: Visual Novel / Text Adventure
visual_novel.promptTemplate:
Design a branching Visual Novel / Text Adventure.

Mechanics:
- Choices: 2-4 per scene, 3+ distinct endings.
- Story length: 8-15 scenes per branch path.
- State tracking: 2-3 hidden flags (e.g., trust, suspicion) toggling certain options.
- Text reveal: 25ms per character typewriter effect, tap to skip.

Mandatory Juice:
- Letter type-in with subtle audio tick every 50ms.
- Choice button: hover lifts 4px, glow border 1px outline.
- Scene transition: 0.4s fade-to-black between locations.
- SFX: Web Audio short noise burst for typing, 440Hz chime for choice picked, 220Hz tone for ending reached.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No walls of text without a choice for 3+ paragraphs.
- Avoid railroad branches that all converge immediately.
- Do not hide back/skip controls.
- Avoid pure aesthetic distraction overshadowing readability.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like 80 Days, atmospheric, decisive, every choice mattering.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Scene graph (JSON or object map)
- [ ] Typewriter renderer
- [ ] Choice buttons rendered dynamically
- [ ] State flag system
- [ ] Multiple endings logic
- [ ] Restart / continue flow

Controls:
Mobile: Tap to advance / select choice. Desktop: Click + Space to advance.

Concrete details below:

reflex.label: Reflex / Reaction
reflex.promptTemplate:
Design a Reflex / Reaction game.

Mechanics:
- Trigger window: 200-800ms randomized 'go' signal after wait period.
- Reaction time captured to ms precision.
- 5-round average displayed.
- False start (input before signal) counts as 1.0s penalty.

Mandatory Juice:
- Pre-go: red full-screen ambient pulse at 1Hz.
- Go signal: instant green flash, 8 ring pop expanding 60px over 0.2s.
- SFX: Web Audio sine 220Hz tone for waiting, sharp 880Hz blip for go, 110Hz buzz for false start.
- Number reveal: time text scales 0->1 in 0.2s elastic ease.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No ambiguous start cue (must be a clear signal).
- Avoid reading-heavy instructions (1 line max).
- Do not include music that masks the audio cue.
- Avoid penalizing the player without showing why.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like a clean reaction tester, precise, addicting, immediately replayable.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Random delay generator
- [ ] Precise time measurement
- [ ] False-start detection
- [ ] Best time + average UI
- [ ] LocalStorage best record
- [ ] Big restart button

Controls:
Mobile: Full-screen tap. Desktop: Spacebar or click.

Concrete details below:

physics_sandbox.label: Physics Sandbox
physics_sandbox.promptTemplate:
Design a Physics Sandbox game using simple Verlet or semi-implicit Euler integration (no Matter.js).

Mechanics:
- Gravity: 980px/s^2 downward.
- Drag-to-aim: line indicator showing trajectory preview (dotted, 8 dots).
- Object types: projectile (player), 3 target objects per level.
- 3 hand-built levels with increasing geometry difficulty.

Mandatory Juice:
- Trajectory preview line, fading dots.
- Impact spark: 10 specks radiating from collision point, 0.3s lifetime.
- Screen shake: 4px for 0.15s on big impact.
- SFX: Web Audio short noise crack for impact, 220Hz pop for target down, 440Hz launch chime.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No complex chained physics joints in v1.
- Avoid invisible collision geometry.
- Do not require pixel-perfect aim (give a tolerance circle).
- Avoid frame-rate-dependent physics (use fixed delta).

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Angry Birds, slingshot anticipation, satisfying chained reactions.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Fixed-timestep physics loop
- [ ] Drag-to-aim input
- [ ] Trajectory preview
- [ ] AABB or circle collision
- [ ] Level select / level transition
- [ ] Win condition per level

Controls:
Mobile: Touch and drag. Desktop: Click and drag.

Concrete details below:

educational.label: Educational Mini-Game
educational.promptTemplate:
Design an Educational Mini-Game for a defined subject and age range (e.g., math drill ages 7-10).

Mechanics:
- Question generation: procedural with adaptive difficulty (easy at start, scales after 3 streak).
- Answer modes: tap multiple choice (3-4 options) or numeric input.
- Streak counter: visible, with milestone celebrations every 5.
- Encouraging feedback: never negative shaming on wrong.

Mandatory Juice:
- Correct: green check pop, 12-spark burst, +10 floating points.
- Wrong: gentle wobble (no shake), correct answer revealed in calm tone.
- SFX: Web Audio sine 880Hz for correct, soft 440Hz for wrong, ascending 3-note chime for milestone.
- Mascot reaction: idle bounce, big smile on streak.

Anti-patterns to avoid:
- No timer pressure that increases anxiety.
- Avoid red/loud failure feedback.
- Do not gate content behind ads.
- Avoid jargon outside the target age range.

Feel like reference line:
Feels like Khan Academy Kids, warm, encouraging, never punishing.

Required features checklist:
- [ ] Adaptive difficulty curve
- [ ] Streak + milestone UI
- [ ] Encouraging messages bank (8+ phrases)
- [ ] Bright child-friendly palette (configurable)
- [ ] LocalStorage best streak
- [ ] Restart / next-set button

Controls:
Mobile: Tap large answer buttons. Desktop: Click + number row keyboard shortcuts.
